results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a brand.

DragonForce launched on BreachForums on December 6, 2023 — not as a tool, but as a cartel. Complete with logo, affiliate portal, and a revenue model that would make a startup founder jealous: 80% to affiliates, 20% to the house.

By March 2025, they weren't just a group — they were an ecosystem.
363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. £300M+ in damage to UK retail alone.

This is what happens when cybercrime gets a brand strategy.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #RaaS #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: A mythic creature built from malware code instantly signals danger and sophistication, stopping the LinkedIn scroll cold.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
The note arrives after everything is already gone.

DragonForce's ransom notes aren't sloppy. They're engineered.
ChaCha8 encryption — cross-platform. Windows. Linux. ESXi. Everything.
BYOVD drivers truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys loaded to blind your EDR before a single file is touched.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Summer 2025.
One cartel. Three household names. £300M+ in damage.

By the time your IR team reads the note, the encryption is done.
The question is never 'how did they get in?' — it's 'why were we still blind?'

#DragonForce #RansomwareAttack #BYOVD #IncidentResponse #CyberThreat
results.hook: A shattered retail trading floor with an actual ransom note mid-decode creates visceral financial dread that every business leader recognizes immediately.

results.caption:
No confirmed nation-state. No arrested operators. No verified identity.

DragonForce claimed Malaysian origins in early messaging — but the cartel structure, the RansomHub absorption on April 8 2025, and the collaboration with Scattered Spider tell a more complicated story.

Scattered Spider brought the social engineering — vishing attacks so convincing they bypassed MFA at three of the UK's most recognized brands.
DragonForce brought the encryption and the infrastructure.

Together: one of the most damaging retail cyber events in British history.

The dragon has no fixed home. That's the point.

#DragonForce #ScatteredSpider #ThreatAttribution #CyberIntelligence #RansomwareCartel
results.hook: Attribution ambiguity visualized as a shadowy war room of masked figures around a globe sparks the unsettling question every CISO asks: who is actually behind this?
